og-title: Night Owl Blend: Best Coffee Beans for Espresso, a Dark Roast Espresso with Chocolate Coffee Beans: a Good No-Acid Coffee — Coffee Beans by James Coffee
product-alt-name: Night Owl Blend Dark Roast Espresso Coffee Beans
product-description: Night Owl Blend is a signature dark roast from James Coffee Co., crafted for drinkers who want a dark, round, immediately comforting espresso with no sharp edges, no bright acidity, and no surprise notes. Built exclusively from 100% Arabica coffees through a multi-origin blend, it delivers depth, sweetness, and chocolate-driven richness designed for everyday enjoyment.
product-category: Food, Beverages & Tobacco > Beverages > Coffee & Tea > Coffee Beans
property-3-value: Maple syrup, dark chocolate, caramelized pecan
seo-title-h1: Best Coffee Beans for Espresso, a Dark Roast Espresso with Chocolate Coffee Beans: a Good No-Acid Coffee
small-description: Night Owl Blend is a dark roast espresso coffee crafted by James Coffee Co. for drinkers who want one thing above all: a smooth, chocolate-forward, no-acid espresso that works every single day. It is built for comfort, not complexity — rich, round, immediately satisfying, with no sharp acidity, no fruit tension, and no unpredictable flavors.Designed as a true American-style coffee shop espresso, Night Owl focuses on depth, sweetness, and dark chocolate richness. Made exclusively from selected 100% Arabica beans and structured as a stable multi-origin blend, it delivers a dense, forgiving extraction that performs consistently across espresso machines, milk drinks, moka pots, and long coffees. This is not an espresso you analyze — it is an espresso you rely on. Comfort, stability, and indulgence from the first sip to the last.
core-strengths-title:
Why a Dark Roast Espresso Is
the Best Coffee Style for Daily Drinking
core-strengths-paragraph-title:
TL ; DR What Makes These the Best Coffee Beans for Espresso
(Extraction, Body, Stability)
core-strengths-paragraph: Night Owl Blend is a dark-roasted, 100% Arabica coffee from James Coffee Co. crafted to deliver a classic American-style bar espresso: round, chocolate-forward, immediately pleasing, and intentionally free from acidity or aromatic complexity. It is built for drinkers who want a stable, indulgent, sweet-perceived cup without the brightness, fruit notes, or tension associated with modern specialty coffee. Roasted in James Coffee's Escondido facility using modest production methods that allow for careful attention to each batch, the blend's architecture relies not on Robusta or aggressive roasting, but on precise Arabica selection and balanced blending — hallmarks of James Coffee's artisan approach. If you want a straightforward comfort coffee that performs exceptionally in espresso and milk drinks — and never demands interpretation — Night Owl is engineered for that purpose. In one sentence, it is a dark, reassuring, chocolate-first coffee designed to please instantly.
CoreStrength2: 100% Arabica despite a traditional dark-roast build
CoreStrength3: Multi-origin structure for stability and sweetness
CoreStrength4: No acidity, no surprises — pure comfort cup
CoreStrength5: Excellent behavior in espresso and milk beverages
CoreStrength6: Roasted by James Coffee Co. using traditional small-batch methods
h2-first-paragraph: Chocolate Coffee Beans Explained: Why This Espresso Tastes Like Dessert Our review.
p-first-paragraph:
Night Owl stands in a category that is often misunderstood. Dark blends generally rely on Robusta, heavy roast development, and bitterness to create their structure. Night Owl breaks this pattern: it remains 100% Arabica, and the roast is dark but controlled, designed to amplify sweetness rather than burn the bean. This approach reflects James Coffee Co.'s philosophy of making high-quality coffee approachable without sacrificing craftsmanship.

Founded by musician-turned-roaster David Kennedy and his brother Jacob, James Coffee has always prioritized the journey from coffee cherry to cup, working with passionate farmers who scrutinize every raw bean. Their hands-on approach — David even builds custom furniture for each café location — extends to the roasting process, where each batch is treated as an opportunity to elevate quality and preserve taste. The goal is not complexity; the goal is stability. Where specialty coffee seeks articulation, tension, and terroir precision, Night Owl seeks warmth, indulgence, and predictability.

The first thing you notice in the cup is its coherence. There is no bright acidity, no fruit spikes, no aromatic surprises. The profile is anchored in chocolate, brown sugar, and toasted nuts — sensory markers associated with caramelization reactions occurring during dark roasting. These reactions convert bean sugars into deeper, syrupy sweetness, creating the maple-syrup, dark-chocolate, and caramelized-pecan character that defines Night Owl. This is not a bean-driven flavor in the specialty sense; it is a roast-driven flavor, intentionally manufactured to be round and comforting.

In espresso, the extraction is dense but never aggressive. The attack is warm, mid-palate syrupy, and the finish soft and cocoa-like. The coffee behaves like a true comfort tool: it forgives imperfect technique, delivers consistent crema, and produces the same stable profile shot after shot. Under milk, the blend transforms into a dessert-like drink — chocolate deepens, caramel becomes creamier, and the nutty notes shift toward praline. For drinkers who value reliability and indulgence more than sensory exploration, Night Owl fulfills its promise with precision.

What stands out in daily use is how approachable the coffee feels. It requires no fine tuning, no interpretation, no intellectual engagement. It fits seamlessly into a morning routine. The low acidity reduces palate fatigue, allowing repeated cups without overstimulation — ideal for people wanting a warm, calm, predictable companion rather than a sensory challenge.
h2-2nd-paragraph:
No Acid Coffee: Why This Espresso Is Easy on the Stomach
Taste & Structure
p-2nd-paragraph: Night Owl is built on three structural decisions that reflect James Coffee Co.'s commitment to quality: 100% Arabica selection, multi-origin blending, and a controlled dark roast. Together, these choices define how the coffee behaves in your grinder, in your machine, and finally in your cup. 100% Arabica means the blend is naturally sweeter and smoother, with less harsh caffeine bite than a Robusta-heavy traditional espresso. For you, this translates into an intensity that feels dense and enveloping, but never scratchy or abrasive on the palate. The multi-origin approach brings another layer of practicality: instead of depending on a single harvest or region, James Coffee Co. composes Night Owl so that chocolate, sugar, and nutty notes remain consistent month after month. If you dial in your espresso recipe once, you are rewarded with a profile that changes very little over time — a crucial element when your routine relies on predictable taste at 7 a.m. The controlled dark roast is the final pillar. Dark roasting raises solubility, which means the coffee is easier to extract across various machines and grind qualities. In real life, this helps if you are using an automatic machine, a basic grinder, or a moka pot: the coffee still gives you body, crema, and flavor without needing competition-level precision. Instead of chasing fruit, florals, or terroir nuance, Night Owl anchors itself in sweetness and chocolate. The result is a blend that behaves like a reliable tool rather than a fragile experiment — a coffee you can trust to deliver the same dark, indulgent comfort whether you drink it as a straight espresso, a cappuccino, or a long mug-style coffee.
h3-1-features: THE ROAST DECISION — DARK BUT CONTROLLED
p-1-features: A dark roast is not just a color; it is a structural decision that shapes every aspect of the cup, from grind behavior to aftertaste. When James Coffee Co. roasts darker, cell walls break down more, internal oils migrate toward the surface, and sugars undergo extended Maillard and caramelization reactions. In practice, this makes Night Owl highly soluble, meaning water can penetrate and dissolve flavor compounds more easily. For you, this broadens the "forgiveness window": slight errors in grind size, tamping, or dose are less catastrophic than they would be with a light-roasted specialty coffee. You still get body and a stable flavor rather than an under-extracted, sour mess. However, pushing a roast too far leads to burnt, ashy bitterness and a dry, hollow mouthfeel. The promise of Night Owl lies exactly in the restraint applied here. Using traditional small-batch roasting methods in their Escondido facility, James Coffee Co. develops the roast long enough to erase bright acidity and highlight dark sugars, but intentionally stops before carbonization dominates. This is why the espresso feels chocolatey and syrupy instead of smoky or charred. You still get the structural bitterness required for an "espresso" sensation, but it is integrated — it frames the sweetness instead of punishing the palate. In daily use, that means you can brew a strong, dark shot that remains comfortable to drink, even without sugar, and that continues to feel soft and approachable when combined with milk.
p-2-features: In the world of dark espresso blends, 100% Arabica is far from the norm. Many roasters rely on Robusta to artificially inflate crema, body, and perceived strength. The trade-off is often higher bitterness, earthy or rubbery notes, and a more aggressive feel in the stomach. James Coffee Co. deliberately rejects this path and builds Night Owl's entire structure on Arabica only — a decision that reflects their commitment to sourcing from passionate farmers who produce the highest quality beans. Arabica beans are naturally higher in sugars and generally lower in caffeine, which gives a smoother, more refined canvas for dark roasting. When these sugars are caramelized, they produce the maple syrup, dark caramel, and brown sugar sensations that define Night Owl's core identity. For the drinker, the 100% Arabica choice means the coffee feels strong without being brutal. You get intensity from the roast, not from coarse bitterness or caffeine shock. This makes a difference over a full day: you can drink multiple espressos or milk drinks without the sensation of being over-saturated or attacked by your coffee. The tactile impression is one of softness and roundness, even in a short, concentrated shot. In terms of flavor, the absence of Robusta also means the cup stays clean: chocolate, nuts, and dark sugar, with no muddy or rubber-like notes in the background. This is what enables Night Owl to sit between supermarket dark roasts and specialty — you retain the comfort and indulgence of a traditional dark profile, but with a much more controlled, polished expression.
p-3-features: A single-origin dark roast can be beautiful for a few weeks, then drift as the harvest changes or the lot runs out. Night Owl takes a different route: James Coffee Co. intentionally builds it from several Arabica origins, each chosen for what it contributes structurally to the blend. One origin may bring body and viscosity, another underlying sweetness, another a clean nutty aftertaste, and yet another depth of cocoa. Roasting and blending are then used to "lock" these roles together into a fixed flavor architecture. This approach reflects the roastery's commitment to "modest production methods" that treat each individual roast as an opportunity to elevate quality. The practical benefit is straightforward: Night Owl tastes like Night Owl, not like "whatever coffee was available this month." For you, as a home drinker or café user, this multi-origin strategy translates into remarkable consistency. Once you dial in your grind setting and recipe — for example, 18 g in, 36 g out, around 25–30 seconds — you can rely on the same general flavor output day after day. If you are running an automatic machine, this is even more valuable: the machine expects a coffee that responds similarly every time, and Night Owl's structure is tuned exactly for that. The blend is also conceived to remain stable under different brew ratios. Whether you pull a classic 1:2 espresso, a slightly longer americano, or even a more concentrated ristretto-style shot, you remain within a narrow, comforting spectrum: chocolate, brown sugar, toasted nuts. You don't have to chase a fleeting "sweet spot"; the coffee is designed to be usable and pleasant across a wide operational range.
h3-4-features: AROMATIC PROFILE — WHAT THE ROAST ACTUALLY PRODUCES
p-4-features: The aromatic profile of Night Owl is not the result of exotic fermentation or experimental processing. It is the direct consequence of roast chemistry applied to sweet, clean Arabica beans sourced by James Coffee Co. from committed farmers worldwide. During roasting, the Maillard reaction — a series of transformations between amino acids and sugars — generates complex molecules that give the impression of toasted nuts, baked goods, and cocoa. As roasting progresses and sugars caramelize, they produce the maple syrup, brown sugar, and dark caramel notes officially listed for Night Owl. This is why, in the cup, the coffee feels like a dessert: the nose evokes chocolate, caramelized nuts, and a hint of warm wood, with no trace of fruit, flowers, or citrus. For the drinker, this has a concrete impact on how the coffee integrates into daily life. If you dislike the idea of tasting "berries" or "grapefruit" in your cup, Night Owl is a safe zone. It never surprises you with unexpected acidity or perfume. As the espresso cools slightly, the profile remains linear: dark chocolate, sugar, nuts. In milk drinks, these notes are amplified — maple syrup becomes more like milky caramel, the pecan character leans toward hazelnut or praline, and the overall impression is that of a liquid dessert rather than a complex gastronomic experience. Even in filter or long coffee formats, the aromatic path stays narrow and reassuring. The cup does not evolve dramatically while cooling; it remains what it is from the first sip to the last: dark, sweet, and constant.
p-5-features: Texture is where many drinkers decide whether a coffee feels "cheap" or "premium" long before they consciously process the flavors. Night Owl's dark roast and blend design work together to build a medium-to-full body with a thick, smooth mouthfeel. The breakdown of cellular structure during roasting, coupled with oil migration, makes the ground coffee more extractable. This is especially important for espresso and moka pot, where water is in contact with the coffee for a short time under pressure. Because Night Owl is easy to extract, it produces a naturally syrupy, dense shot even when the grind or dose is not perfect. On the palate, this translates into a warm, enveloping sensation right from the attack. The first impression is often "sweet and thick" rather than sharp or thin. Mid-palate, the flavor feels like chocolate syrup or dark caramel, coating the tongue instead of skimming over it. The finish brings a soft bitterness with cocoa and toasted nuts, but the texture never collapses into dryness. For the user, this means the coffee feels satisfying in small volumes (straight espresso) and equally capable of standing up to dilution in larger drinks (americano, long coffee) without becoming watery. In milk drinks, the dense base integrates seamlessly with microfoam, creating cappuccinos and lattes that taste structured and indulgent rather than hollow. Whether you drink it black or with milk, you experience a tactile feeling of substance — a sense that your coffee is "there," not just aromatics floating in hot water.
p-6-features: Bitterness is one of the most misunderstood components of coffee. Completely removing it would make the drink feel unfinished; letting it dominate makes it undrinkable. James Coffee Co. treats bitterness as a structural tool rather than a byproduct in Night Owl. During the dark roast, melanoidins and other compounds responsible for cocoa-like, dark-chocolate bitterness are developed deliberately, while extreme carbonization — the source of burnt, ashy bitterness — is avoided. In the cup, this produces a finish that clearly signals "espresso" but without scratching the throat or drying the tongue. For the drinker, the result is a coffee that feels decisive, not timid, yet remains easy to finish. You can drink a straight shot without sugar and still perceive balance: initial sweetness, syrupy mid-palate, and a clean, slightly bitter tail that invites the next sip instead of punishing it. In milk drinks, that same structural bitterness is essential. It cuts through the fat and lactose, preventing the beverage from turning into something flabby and indistinct. The chocolate notes stay defined, the caramel stays lively, and you avoid the cloying sensation that overly sweet blends can produce. Over the course of a day, controlled bitterness also reduces palate fatigue. You get enough structure to feel awake and satisfied, but not so much harshness that a second or third cup becomes unpleasant. In simple terms, Night Owl gives you the "espresso bite" you expect, but in a form that supports sweetness, texture, and comfort rather than fighting against them.
h2-2nd-paragraph-realworld:
Good Coffee Without Complexity: The Comfort Coffee Philosophy
Real-World Performance
p-1-features-realworld: In daily brewing, Night Owl is defined by stability — a quality that reflects James Coffee Co.'s commitment to producing consistent, reliable coffee for everyday enjoyment. It extracts consistently across methods — espresso, moka pot, filter, or automatic machines — because its solubility profile is high and its acidity is low. This means the coffee does not punish the user for slight inconsistencies in grind size, temperature, or recipe. Every cup falls within the same spectrum: chocolate, caramel, toasted nuts. As espresso, it produces a steady, dense crema and a linear flavor curve. There is no evolution toward fruit, no sudden spikes of acidity, no aromatic volatility. This predictability makes it an ideal everyday coffee, especially for people who want a reliable morning anchor — exactly the kind of approachable, high-quality experience that James Coffee Co. was founded to provide. In milk drinks, Night Owl reaches its full potential. Milk integrates with caramelized sugars and dark chocolate compounds, amplifying sweetness and adding a dessert-like quality. Cappuccinos become velvety, lattes taste like chocolate-caramel beverages, and flat whites maintain a clean finish without acidity disturbing the milk texture. This performance makes Night Owl a popular choice at James Coffee's San Diego café locations. In long coffees or filter use, the cup remains calm, low-acid, and stable from start to finish. There is little aromatic evolution — a deliberate choice. The flavor remains constant even as the temperature drops, providing reassurance rather than exploration. The absence of acidity also reduces palate fatigue. Some specialty coffees are stimulating, intense, and mentally engaging. Night Owl is the opposite: it is a comfort beverage meant to soothe, not to challenge. For many drinkers, this makes it easier to drink multiple cups a day without sensory overload.
h2-comparisons:
Night Owl Blend vs Specialty Coffee vs Supermarket Dark Roast
Comparisons
p-1-comparison: Although both may be described as "chocolatey," Night Owl and a specialty chocolate-forward coffee sit on two completely different philosophical and technical foundations. In specialty coffee, chocolate notes emerge naturally from the bean itself — from its terroir, varietal genetics, altitude, processing method, and post-harvest fermentation. This means the chocolate flavor is not imposed but revealed. It is often layered, evolving, and tied to the identity of the place where the coffee was grown. A specialty chocolate-forward espresso may begin with cocoa, shift toward sweet plum or brown sugar mid-palate, and finish with a soft nutty or fruity echo. There is movement, nuance, and a sense that the coffee is telling you a story about its origin. Night Owl, by design, takes the opposite route. Its chocolate is created through roast development by James Coffee Co., not terroir articulation. Instead of a layered, unfolding profile, you get an immediate, enveloping, dessert-like chocolate presence that is stable from sip to sip. For the drinker, this difference matters: specialty chocolatey coffees require more precise dialing-in, more attention to brew ratios, and more openness to variation as the coffee evolves in the cup. Night Owl does not demand this. Its chocolate is warm, thick, and constant — engineered to deliver comfort, not complexity. If specialty coffee teaches the palate, Night Owl reassures it. If specialty chocolate is a narrative, Night Owl is a blanket. In practical terms, the choice depends on your intention as a drinker. If you want to explore nuance, terroir, and evolving sweetness, a specialty chocolate-forward coffee is the right path. If you want predictability, indulgence, and a chocolate profile that behaves the same at 6 a.m. every day regardless of grind precision, Night Owl is the more suitable companion.
p-2-comparison: Supermarket dark roasts often aim for intensity but sacrifice clarity and freshness in the process. Many rely on Robusta-heavy blends for crema and caffeine punch, but this brings earthy, rubbery, or woody off-notes that mask sweetness. Storage conditions — months in warehouses, bright supermarket lighting, inconsistent packaging — accelerate oxidation. Rancid oils develop, aromatic volatility is lost, and the cup becomes dominated by dryness and burnt bitterness. Even when freshly opened, many supermarket coffees carry a dull, cardboard-like aroma caused by staling and poor-quality raw material. Night Owl is built to avoid every one of these pitfalls. Roasted in small batches by James Coffee Co. in their Escondido facility, its 100% Arabica composition ensures cleaner sweetness and a smoother structure. The roast is controlled, meaning bitterness is present but integrated — not sharp, metallic, or aggressive. Because the blend is made from higher-quality lots sourced from committed farmers, the base flavors remain coherent: chocolate, dark sugar, toasted nuts. You never encounter the "mystery bitterness" or stale aroma common in industrially roasted beans. For the drinker, the impact is immediate. Night Owl tastes cleaner, fresher, and more articulate despite being a dark roast. Where supermarket coffee often collapses in milk, producing a muddy, indistinct beverage, Night Owl gains clarity: chocolate becomes more defined, caramel becomes creamier, and the cup feels intentional rather than accidental. In long coffees, Night Owl remains stable and drinkable, while supermarket blends typically reveal harshness as dilution exposes their flaws. In short: supermarket coffee delivers darkness; Night Owl from James Coffee Co. delivers dark flavor. Darkness alone is easy; dark flavor — sweet, structured, and controlled — requires craft.
h3-3-comparison: NIGHT OWL VS ITALIAN-STYLE DARK ROASTS
p-3-comparison: Italian roasts occupy a distinct tradition: heavy development, strong bitterness, and a sharp, smoky finish designed to cut through sugar and milk. Many Italian espressos rely on a significant proportion of Robusta to increase crema, boost caffeine strength, and create the signature punch Italians historically associate with espresso. The result is often a cup with high bitterness, minimal sweetness, and a lingering charred or smoky aftertaste. For certain drinkers, this is the essence of "espresso." But for others, it can feel aggressive, drying, and fatiguing over repeated cups. Night Owl positions itself differently. James Coffee Co. keeps the comforting depth associated with darker roasts but shifts toward the American-style coffee shop profile: sweeter, rounder, and more chocolate-driven, with much less smokiness and none of the rubbery edges that extensive Robusta use can introduce. Instead of sharp intensity, you get a soft, syrupy density. Instead of a towering, bitter finish, you get a gentle cocoa aftertaste. For the user, this repositioning has important implications. If you enjoy milk drinks, Night Owl integrates more gracefully: bitterness lifts the sweetness without overpowering it, and the chocolate base harmonizes with milk rather than fighting it. Italian-style roasts can dominate milk; Night Owl collaborates with it. If you drink straight espresso, Night Owl is far less abrasive. You can enjoy multiple shots a day without palate fatigue, whereas traditional Italian blends often demand sugar or risk overwhelming the senses. Ultimately, Night Owl keeps the emotional comfort of a dark roast while removing the harsh edges. It offers the depth of tradition, but with the smoothness and accessibility of a modern, carefully engineered dark espresso from an artisan American roaster. In short: Night Owl is not specialty coffee, but it is far above supermarket dark roasts in cleanliness, sweetness, and stability — a testament to James Coffee Co.'s handcrafted approach and quality sourcing.
h2-proscons:
Pros

Pros5: You want dependable flavor every day
Pros6: You dislike surprises in your cup
Pros7: You value artisan roasting and independent roasters
h2-cons:
Cons
Cons of a Chocolate-Forward Dark Roast Espresso
Cons6: May feel too simple for specialty coffee enthusiasts seeking bean-driven complexity
h2-shouldbuy: Who This Coffee Is For (Beginners, Daily Drinkers, Milk Lovers)
p-shouldbuy: Night Owl is designed for drinkers who want reliability, indulgence, and a dark, sweet experience without the learning curve of specialty coffee. Roasted by James Coffee Co., an independent San Diego roaster with a commitment to handcrafted quality and ethical sourcing, this blend represents accessible excellence. If you prefer calm, chocolatey, caramel-driven cups — especially in espresso or milk drinks — it excels. If you seek brightness, fruit complexity, or evolving aromatics, Night Owl will feel too simple. It is a comfort coffee, not a reflective or exploratory one. For many, that is precisely what makes it valuable — and supporting an artisan roaster like James Coffee Co. adds another layer of satisfaction to your morning ritual.

h2-faq: FAQ: Dark Roast Espresso, Acidity, Bitterness & Milk Drinks
faq-q1: What coffee bean is best for espresso?
faq-a1: Arabica beans with medium to dark roast, low acidity, and high solubility are best for espresso.
faq-q2: What coffee beans are best for espresso?
faq-a2: Beans designed for espresso extraction with balanced body, sweetness, and controlled bitterness.
faq-q3: What are the best coffee beans for espresso?
faq-a3: The best beans are those that extract consistently and perform well under pressure, especially in milk drinks.
faq-q4: What is the best coffee for espresso?
faq-a4: The best espresso coffee is one that is stable, low-acid, chocolate-forward, and forgiving to extract.
faq-q5: What is the best espresso coffee machine for the home?
faq-a5: The best home espresso machine is one with stable temperature control, good pressure regulation, and a quality grinder.
faq-a6: Dark roast coffee is roasted longer to develop bold, chocolatey, smoky, and low-acid characteristics.
faq-a7: In flavor, yes. In caffeine, not necessarily.

faq-a8: No. Dark roast usually contains slightly less caffeine than light roast by bean weight.

faq-a15: Yes. Dark roast typically feels smoother and less aggressive on the stomach.

ResellersTitle: Find where to buy Night Owl Blend
ManufacturerTitle: Night Owl Blend Roaster — James Coffee Co.
ManufacturerDescription: James Coffee Co. is an independently owned and operated coffee roaster based in San Diego County, California, specializing in handcrafted artisan roasted coffee. Founded in 2013-2014 by brothers David and Jacob Kennedy (along with David's wife Carina), the company began when David — a guitarist for Angels & Airwaves and Box Car Racer — started roasting coffee in a popcorn maker on his porch after being inspired by Australia's specialty coffee scene. What started as a passion project in their garage evolved into a thriving family business with a roastery in Escondido and multiple café locations across San Diego. James Coffee Co. is known for its commitment to traditional small-batch roasting methods, ethical sourcing from farmers who support their communities, and creating a vibrant coffee community. David's hands-on approach extends beyond the coffee — he custom-builds furniture for each café location, adding a personal touch to every space. The company offers a revolving assortment of distinctive single-origin beans as well as exclusive James Coffee crafted blends, including Night Owl. Using modest production methods, they approach each individual roast as an opportunity to elevate and preserve quality and taste. From the diligent hand that picks the coffee cherry to the thorough process of roasting, James Coffee strives to instill confidence that the conclusion of your coffee's journey will be a fantastic cup. James Coffee Co. represents the best of American artisan coffee culture: independent, handcrafted, community-focused, and accessible without compromising on quality.
